subject: Economics
chapter: Simple Applications of Tools of Demand and Supply

remember: Students will be able to recall the concept of demand and supply and their determinants.
Students will be able to explain the law of demand and supply and their graphical representation.
Students will be able to define the concept of equilibrium price and quantity.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of change in demand and supply and its effect on equilibrium price and quantity.
Students will be able to explain the concept of price ceiling and price floor and their effect on market equilibrium.
Students will be able to understand the concept of consumer's and producer's surplus and their significance in economic analysis.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of demand and supply to real-life situations and make informed decisions.
Students will be able to use the concept of equilibrium price and quantity to analyze market trends and make predictions.
Students will be able to calculate consumer's and producer's surplus in different market scenarios.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the impact of changes in demand and supply on market equilibrium and consumer welfare.
Students will be able to evaluate the effect of government policies on market equilibrium and consumer welfare.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the concept of demand and supply in different markets and industries.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT, the objectives of this chapter are to understand the concept of demand and supply, their determinants, and the law of demand and supply.
To explain the concept of equilibrium price and quantity and their significance in market analysis.
To understand the concept of change in demand and supply and its effect on market equilibrium.
board_exam_objectives: To test the student's understanding of the concept of demand and supply and their determinants.
To assess the student's ability to explain the law of demand and supply and their graphical representation.
To evaluate the student's understanding of the concept of equilibrium price and quantity and their significance in market analysis.
